claim: mpociot/claude-code-slack-bot wraps the Claude Code SDK in a TypeScript Socket Mode bot with streaming responses, file uploads, MCP server integration, and AWS Bedrock/Vertex AI backends; 157 stars, last pushed 2025-06-27 (stale ~10 months as of April 2026).
quote: Connect your local Claude Code agent with Slack. Language: TypeScript. stargazers_count: 157. pushed_at: 2025-06-27T15:21:04Z.

claim: AnandChowdhary/claude-code-slack-bot takes a different approach — runs on Cloudflare Workers (serverless), creates GitHub issues from Slack mentions, and delegates execution to the official Claude Code GitHub Action while polling progress back to Slack threads; 11 stars, last pushed 2025-09-04.
quote: 🤖 Use Claude Code from a Slack bot, built on top of Claude Code Action. stargazers_count: 11. pushed_at: 2025-09-04T15:39:26Z. Workers + KV + Queues handles Slack event webhooks and GitHub API interactions.

claim: yuya-takeyama/cc-slack is a Go bot that exposes Claude Code via a `/cc` slash command with single- and multi-directory project modes, thread-based session continuity, and image handling; 14 stars, last pushed 2025-08-23 (modestly maintained).
quote: Interact with Claude Code on Slack. Language: Go. stargazers_count: 14. pushed_at: 2025-08-23T11:21:46Z. Slash command interface (/cc default), thread-based conversations.

claim: chenhg5/cc-connect is by far the most popular bridge (6,422 stars, last pushed 2026-04-27 — actively maintained), a Go binary that uses Slack Socket Mode (no public IP needed) plus 10 other chat platforms and 10+ agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI), with stateful sessions and a web admin dashboard.
quote: Bridge local AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Codex) to messaging platforms… stargazers_count: 6422. pushed_at: 2026-04-27T12:55:54Z. Uses Slack's Socket Mode connection… eliminates the need for a public-facing webhook.

claim: JessyTsui/Claude-Code-Remote (1,228 stars, JavaScript, last pushed 2025-12-06) controls Claude Code via email/Telegram/LINE/desktop using a hook + tmux/PTY command-injection mechanism; Slack is on the TODO roadmap, not yet shipped.
quote: Control Claude Code remotely via email、discord、telegram… roadmap lists 'Discord Integration' and 'Slack Workflow' as planned features under 'TODO List'. stargazers_count: 1228. pushed_at: 2025-12-06.

claim: dbenn8/claude-slack (22 stars, Python, last pushed 2026-01-31) implements bidirectional Slack ↔ Claude using a hook-based pattern — `.claude/hooks/` notifies Slack and a separate `slack_listener.py` Socket Mode bot writes replies to a shared file polled via `/check`; multi-session phase 2.5 in progress.
quote: Universal Slack integration for Claude Code - bidirectional communication, multi-session support, and real-time messaging. stargazers_count: 22. Language: Python. pushed_at: 2026-01-31T08:45:46Z.

claim: takafu/slack-claude-bot (TypeScript, 2 stars, last pushed 2026-01-16) is a thin per-thread Claude Code CLI wrapper where each Slack thread holds its own session context — a minimal but recently active reference implementation.
quote: Slack bot that integrates with Claude Code CLI for AI-powered responses. stargazers_count: 2. Language: TypeScript. pushed_at: 2026-01-16T13:54:36Z.

claim: engineers-hub-ltd's slack-claude-code-integration (JavaScript, 11 stars, last pushed 2025-06-30) wraps Claude Code as an MCP server (Slack → Bot → MCP server → Claude Code CLI) with a `/claude` slash command and `--project` flag for multi-project switching.
quote: Architecture Flow: Slack → Slack Bot → Claude Code MCP Server → Claude Code CLI… stargazers_count: 11. pushed_at: 2025-06-30T07:26:48Z.

claim: satetsu888/slackbot-for-claude (TypeScript, 5 stars, last pushed 2024-03-17) is effectively abandoned — predates Claude Code and just wraps the raw Anthropic API; included only as historical context.
quote: Slackbot that integrates with the Claude API. stargazers_count: 5. pushed_at: 2024-03-17T13:42:14Z.

claim: Anthropic ships an official first-party Claude Code in Slack integration that auto-detects coding intent in @Claude mentions, opens a Claude Code on the web session, and creates one PR per session against a GitHub repo using the user's individual Claude plan rate limits — making most community 'remote control' bots redundant for the basic case.
quote: When you mention @Claude with a coding task, Claude automatically detects the intent and creates a Claude Code session on the web… each session can create one pull request, sessions use your individual Claude plan's rate limits.

claim: OpenACP is another self-hosted bridge with an interactive setup wizard supporting Telegram, Discord, and Slack simultaneously, daemon/foreground modes, and per-workspace agent defaults — targeted at users who want to drive Claude Code from any phone client without a public IP.
quote: An interactive setup wizard asks which platform you want (Telegram, Discord, Slack, or all three), your bot token, which workspace directory to use, which agent to default to, and whether to run in foreground or daemon mode.
